description:
Make a raw authenticated request against the API.

Honors --dry-run (prints the equivalent curl) and --output. Use it for endpoints the
typed resource commands don't cover yet.
Examples:
  lsqueezy api GET /products -q 'page[size]=1'
  lsqueezy api POST /checkouts -d @checkout.json

args.description:
Positional command line arguments
Usage pattern: <METHOD> <PATH>

dry-run.description: print the equivalent curl and make no request

sort.description: JSON:API sort field, prefix with - for desc (list commands)

name: lsqueezy_api

description:
Create a checkout.

Supply attributes with --data (raw JSON object or @file) and/or repeated --set
key=value, and relationships with repeated --rel name=type:id. The JSON:API
envelope (type/attributes/relationships) is added for you.
Examples:
  lsqueezy checkouts create --data '{"name":"Acme"}' --rel store=stores:1
  lsqueezy checkouts create --set name=Acme --set email=a@b.co --rel store=stores:1 --dry-run

args.description: Positional command line arguments

data.description: attributes as a JSON object, or @file

dry-run.description: print the equivalent curl and make no request

sort.description: JSON:API sort field, prefix with - for desc (list commands)
